title: Pick encodings by cardinality and model type
description: Match categorical encoding to feature cardinality and the downstream model so representation does not waste capacity or leak.
body: Use when turning categorical fields into model inputs. Do not one-hot a high-cardinality field for a linear model — it explodes dimensionality and starves the signal; and do not target-encode without out-of-fold computation. Actions: one-hot low-cardinality fields, use ordinal only for true orderings, learn embeddings for high-cardinality fields in neural models, and compute any target encoding out-of-fold. Validation signals include an encoded matrix whose width is sane for the model and a benchmark comparing encodings on validation. Risk boundary: leaky target encoding inflates metrics; if encoding statistics are fit on the full set, treat it as leakage and refit out-of-fold.
